metadata.summary: An extension to the `star_ray` package that uses XML as a description language for the state of an environment.

documentation:
# `star-ray-pygame`

[![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)

The `star_ray_pygame` package is an extension of [`star_ray`](https://github.com/dicelab-rhul/star-ray) that implements UI functionality backed by the `pygame` and `star_ray_xml` packages.

`star_ray_pygame` implements a specific type of `Agent` known as an `Avatar` which represents a user (typically human) who is interacting with the environment and the agents within it. The UI backend is `pygame`, and `svg` (Scalable Vector Graphics) are used to render the UI. The UI state completely integrated with the state of the environment allowing other agents to view and potentially modify it. This is a dramatic departure from some common software engineering practices such as the MVC (or similar) design pattern. The UI is NOT decoupled from the program state - in fact, for the most part it IS the program state. This package relies on another extension `star_ray_xml` to manage the state of the UI (as well as any other state variables).

This extension has been developed with the intention of exploring human-agent interactions. Agents are able to view a structured version of the UI and modify it as a means of interacting with the user in the most general way. They are able to effectively render text and graphics via their actions. The (human) user may take similar action (via their peripheral devices - mouse, keyboard, etc.) to communicate back (according to whatever functionality the UI is exposing). Agents are able to listen to these user inputs and make futher decisions based on them.

This package serves as a very flexible foundation for supporting researching into human-agent (or AI) interactions on digital devices by leveraging some existing technologies.

See for example, the [`icua`](https://github.com/dicelab-rhul/icua) project.

## Related Projects

- [star-ray](https://github.com/dicelab-rhul/star-ray)
- [icua](https://github.com/dicelab-rhul/icua)

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.utils
documentation: Module contain various utilities used in the `star_ray_pygame` package.

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.actuator
documentation: This module defines a simple actuator `AvatarActuator` intended to be used by an `Avatar` that will simply forward any user input to the environment.

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.actuator.AvatarActuator

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.actuator.AvatarActuator.attempt
documentation:
Default attempt method, simply forwards any `UserInputEvent`s to the environment (assuming the use of a `star_ray.agent.AgentRouted`).

Args:
    action (UserInputEvent): to forward

Returns:
    UserInputEvent: the action (returning the action is default attempt method behaviour).

This simple actuator will automatically foward all user input events (see `star_ray_pygame.UserInputEvent` for a full list) to the environment. This allows other agents to subscribe to receive these events, or for them to be processed by the `Ambient`.

The `star_ray_pygame` package is an extension of [`star_ray`](https://github.com/dicelab-rhul/star-ray) that implements UI functionality backed by the `pygame` and `star_ray_xml` packages.

`star_ray_pygame` implements a specific type of `Agent` known as an `Avatar` which represents a user (typically human) who is interacting with the environment and the agents within it. The UI backend is `pygame`, and `svg` (Scalable Vector Graphics) are used to render the UI. The UI state completely integrated with the state of the environment allowing other agents to view and potentially modify it. This is a dramatic departure from some common software engineering practices such as the MVC (or similar) design pattern. The UI is NOT decoupled from the program state - in fact, for the most part it IS the program state. This package relies on another extension `star_ray_xml` to manage the state of the UI (as well as any other state variables).

This extension has been developed with the intention of exploring human-agent interactions. Agents are able to view a structured version of the UI and modify it as a means of interacting with the user in the most general way. They are able to effectively render text and graphics via their actions. The (human) user may take similar action (via their peripheral devices - mouse, keyboard, etc.) to communicate back (according to whatever functionality the UI is exposing). Agents are able to listen to these user inputs and make futher decisions based on them.

This package serves as a very flexible foundation for supporting researching into human-agent (or AI) interactions on digital devices by leveraging some existing technologies.

See for example, the [`icua`](https://github.com/dicelab-rhul/icua) project.

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar
documentation: Module defines the `Avatar` class, see class for details.

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    sensors (list[Sensor], optional): list of sensors. Defaults to None. An `XMLSensor` will always be added to this list.
    actuators (list[Actuator], optional): list of actuators. Defaults to None.
    window_config (WindowConfiguration | None, optional): window configuration for the pygame window. Defaults to None, see default in `star_ray_pygame.View`.
    kwargs (dict[str,Any]): additional optional arguments.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.xml_sensor
documentation:
Getter for the `XMLSensor` that is attached to this agent.

Returns:
    XMLSensor: the sensor

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.get_screen_info
documentation:
Get information about the screen (monitor), including the monitor index and size.

NOTE: Currently only single monitor setups are supported, the monitor index will always be zero.

Returns:
    dict[str,Any]: screen information

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.get_window_info
documentation:
Get information about the display window, including: title, position (on the monitor) and size (in pixels).

Returns:
    dict[str,Any]: window information

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.on_error_observation
documentation:
Callback for error observations, by default an exception is raised. Override for custom handling of these errors.

Args:
    observation (ErrorObservation): observation containing the error.

Raises:
    observation.exception: the exception contained in the observation (raised by default).

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.on_observation
documentation:
Callback when receiving an observation. By default this is only expected to be called upon the first observation made by the attached `XMLSensor` which senses the full state of the environment (all svg code).

Args:
    observation (ActiveObservation): observation containing svg code for rendering.
    component (Component): component that the observation originated from

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar._on_xml_change
documentation: Called whenever an `XMLQuery` event is received via the `XMLSensor` attached to this agent, it will update the state of the view.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.on_event
documentation:
Automatically route all events that may come from sensors to relevant actuators.

Args:
    observation (Event): observations to route

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.render
documentation: Render the UI.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.avatar.Avatar.get_screen_size
documentation:
Get the screen/monitor size (in pixels).

Returns:
    tuple[int, int]: screen size

Class implementing an `Agent` that acts on behalf of a (human) user. It displays a UI that the user can interact with. User input (clicks, key presses, etc.) will be captured and executed via this agents actuators. Such actuators will typically convert these user input events to actions that will mutate the environment in some way, or request new information to be displayed in the UI. It is also typical for user input events to be made avaliable to other agents in the environment (via `star_ray`s pub-sub mechanism), this can be done simply by forwarding them directly to the environment via a dedicated actuator.

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.event
documentation: Module defining all user input event types and other userful event types such as XML query primitives. Many are collected from `star_ray` and exposed here. Notably the `MouseButtonEvent` and `MouseMotionEvent` have been redefined with additional fields. See these classes for details.

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.event.MouseButtonEvent
documentation: An extension of the original `star_ray.event.MouseButtonEvent` which now operates in SVG space by default. The raw (pixel) position is instead stored in the `position_raw` field.

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.event.MouseMotionEvent
documentation: An extension of the original `star_ray.event.MouseMotionEvent` which now operates in SVG space by default. The raw (pixel) position is instead stored in the `position_raw` field, and similarly for the relative position in `relative_raw`.

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient
documentation: The module defines the `SVGAmbient` class which is an extension of `star_ray_xml.XMLAmbient`. This ambient supports pub-sub of XML queries, and contains some useful functionality regarding the handling of SVG data.

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

This will create a root SVG canvas to which all other elements will be drawn. The root svg element id attribute is `root`. This can be changed with an `update` query is desired.

Args:
    agents (list[Agent]): list of agents to add to this Ambient initially.
    svg_size (tuple[float, float] | None, optional): size of the root svg element. Defaults to None, meaning `width` and `height` will remain undefined.
    svg_position (tuple[float, float] | None, optional): position of the root svg element. Defaults to None, which sets both `x` and `y` to zero.
    svg_namespaces (dict[str, str] | None, optional): the namespaces used. Defaults to the standard svg namespace URI mapping (see `SVGAmbient.DEFAULT_SVG_NAMESPACES`).
    terminate_on_window_close (bool, optional): whether to terminate the environment if a `WindowClosedEvent` occurs, this typically means the user has exited the simulation. Defaults to True.
    kwargs (dict[str,Any]): additional optional arguments.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient._initialise_root
documentation: Initialise attributes of the root svg element.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.on_xml_event
documentation:
A callback that is triggered immediately AFTER an XMLQuery is executed in `__update__`.

Args:
    action (XMLQuery): the action

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.on_user_input_event
documentation:
Callback that is triggered when a user input action is executed in `__update__`. The type hint `UserInputEvent` is used to determine which user input events can be processed by this ambient, it MUST be provided by an override of this method.

Args:
    action (UserInputEvent): action

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.on_unknown_event
documentation:
Callback that is triggered when an action of unknown type is executed in `__update__`. This may be overriden for custom handling of such events. Otherwise a ValueError will be raised.

Args:
    action (Event): unknown action

Raises:
    ValueError: raised by default.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.on_exit_event
documentation:
Callback that is triggered when a `WindowCloseEvent` is executed in `__update__`. By default this will flag the environment from termination. This behaviour can be overriden in a subclass.

Args:
    action (WindowCloseEvent): window close action

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.__subscribe__
documentation:
Subscribe to receive XMLQuery events from this environment. This will be called automatically when the relevant actions are executed and should not be called manually. Supported actions are only those that inherit from `star_ray_xml.XMLQuery`.

Args:
    action (Subscribe | Unsubscribe): the subscription action

Raises:
    TypeError: if the action is not a subscription action

Returns:
    ActiveObservation | ErrorActiveObservation: the resulting observation - confirmation that the subscription was successful (or not).

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.ambient.SVGAmbient.__update__
documentation:
Execute a write action in this `Ambient`.

Supported action types:
- Any action that inherits from `XMLQuery`
- User input actions (types specified by the type hint in `SVGAmbient.on_user_input_event`, see method docs for details.)
- `WindowCloseEvent` if `terminate_on_window_close` was set to True.

Unsupported actions will be routed to `SVGAmbient.on_unknown_event`, which may raise an exception.

To support additional action types override this method (make sure to call `super().__update__`).
To support additional user input action type override`on_user_input_event` and add new types to the type hints
- it is best to keep existing type hints unless you explicitly want them to be treated as unknown types.

Args:
    action (Any): action

Returns:
    ActiveObservation | ErrorActiveObservation: the resulting observation
An `Ambient` implementation that provides some useful SVG related functionality, as well as pub-sub support for SVG related queries.

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface
documentation: Module that defines the backend for svg rendering to a pygame surface. This relies on the cairosvg package.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.point_in_rect
documentation: Determine if a point is inside a rectangle.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.point_in_circle
documentation: Determine if a point is inside a circle.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.parse_transform
documentation: Parses an svg `transform` attribute extracting the `scale`, `rotation` and `translation`.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.in_svg
documentation: Checks if the point is in the bounds of the svg element `node` and returns this aswell as the point transformed to the nodes coordinate space.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.in_group
documentation: Checks if the point is in the bounds of the `group` element and returns this aswell as the point transformed to the nodes coordinate space.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.elements_under
documentation:
Gets all svg elements ids that are under the given `point`. Note that not all svg transformation are supported.

Args:
    node: the SVG element to check.
    point (Tuple[float, float]): to check under

Returns:
    List[str]: list of element ids that are under the `point`.

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    surface_size (tuple[int,int]) : size of the render surface.
    _debug (bool, optional): whether to enable debug mode, this will render debug information to the UI. Defaults to False.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.update
documentation:
Update internal svg data.

Args:
    svg_tree (ET.ElementBase): root of the svg tree

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.svg_size
documentation:
Getter for the size of the root svg element (svg coordinates).

Returns:
    tuple[float,float]: root svg element size

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.surface_position
documentation:
Getter for the position of the surface in the window, this is used to place the surface in the window.

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: surface position in the window.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.surface_size
documentation:
Getter for the size of the render surface (in pixels).

Returns:
    tuple[int, int]: size of the render surface.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.scaling_factor
documentation:
Getter for the scaling factor (maintaining aspect ratio) of the svg to ensure it fits in the render surface.

Returns:
    float: scaling factor

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.pixel_to_svg
documentation:
Transforms a point from pixel space to svg space.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): to transform

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: transformed point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.pixel_scale_to_svg_scale
documentation:
Scale a point (typically a (width, height) pair or similar) that lies in pixel space to svg space. Only scaling is applied.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): point to scale

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: scaled point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.svg_to_pixel
documentation:
Transform a point from svg space to pixel space.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): to transform

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: transformed point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.svg_scale_to_pixel_scale
documentation:
Scale a point (typically a (width, height) pair or similar) that lies in svg space to pixel space. Only scaling is applied.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): point to scale

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: scaled point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.render
documentation:
Render the svg to the pygame surface `window`.

Args:
    window (pygame.Surface): pygame surface
    background_color (str, optional): background color. Defaults to white.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.render_to_array
documentation:
Render this cairo surface directly to a numpy array of the given size. `size` serves the role of the window size in the usual rendering process, it is assumed to be >= to the svg surface size.

Args:
    size (tuple[int, int]): array size to render to (>= svg surface size).

Returns:
    np.ndarray: the resulting array in WHC (uint8) format

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface._surface_to_npim
documentation: Transforms a Cairo `surface` into a numpy array.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface._svg_to_npim
documentation: Renders a svg bytestring as a RGB image in a numpy array.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.cairosurface.CairoSVGSurface.elements_under
documentation:
Gets all svg element `id`s that are under the given `point`.

Args:
    point (Tuple[float, float]): to check under, expected svg space unless `transform=True` in which case window space (pixels) is expected.
    transform (bool, optional): whether to transform the given `point` to svg space. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    List[str]: list of element ids that are under the `point`.
A surface implementation that is able to render svg graphics to a pygame window.

modules.name: star_ray_pygame.view
documentation: Module that defines the `View` class, this class is used to render SVG in a `pygame` window.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.get_screen_size
documentation: Getter for the screen size. This will typically not change during the course of the simulation.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_window_move_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `WindowMoveEvent` from a `pygame` window move event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_window_focus_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `WindowFocusEvent` from a `pygame` window focus event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_window_resize_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `WindowResizeEvent` from a `pygame` window resize event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_screen_size_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `ScreenSizeEvent` from a `pygame` screen size event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_window_close_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates an `WindowCloseEvent` from a `pygame` window close event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_window_open_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates an `WindowOpenEvent` from a `pygame` window open event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_key_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `KeyEvent` instance from a `pygame` keyboard event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_mouse_button_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `MouseButtonEvent` instance from a `pygame` mouse event.

functions.name: star_ray_pygame.view.create_mouse_motion_event_from_pygame_event
documentation: Creates a `MouseMotionEvent` instance from a `pygame` mouse movement event.

variables.name: star_ray_pygame.view.PYGAME_WINDOWCLOSE
documentation: Alias for PYGAME_QUIT

classes.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    window_config (WindowConfiguration): configuration of the pygame window.
    surface_size (tuple[int, int], optional): size of the render surface (in pixels). Defaults to None, meaning it will be derived from the supplied window size.
    _debug (bool, optional): whether to enable debug mode, this will render debug information to the UI. Defaults to False.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.get_window_info
documentation:
Get information about the display window, including: title, position (on the monitor) and size (in pixels).

Returns:
    dict[str,Any]: window information

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.get_window_aspect
documentation:
Get the aspect ratio of the window.

Returns:
    float: aspect ratio

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.get_screen_info
documentation:
Get information about the screen (monitor), including the monitor index and size.

NOTE: Currently only single monitor setups are supported, the monitor index will always be zero.

Returns:
    dict[str,Any]: screen information

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.update
documentation:
Update the internal svg in preparation for rendering. This will typically preceed a call to `View.render`.

Args:
    svg_tree (ET.ElementBase): root of the svg tree.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.render
documentation: Render svg to the window. This will typically follow a call to `View.update`.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.elements_under
documentation:
Gets all svg element `id`s that are under the given `point`.

Args:
    point (Tuple[float, float]): to check under, expected svg space unless `transform=True` in which case window space (pixels) is expected.
    transform (bool, optional): whether to transform the given `point` to svg space. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    List[str]: list of element ids that are under the `point`.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.pixel_to_svg
documentation:
Transforms a point from pixel space to svg space.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): to transform

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: transformed point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.svg_to_pixel
documentation:
Transform a point from svg space to pixel space.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): to transform

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: transformed point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.pixel_scale_to_svg_scale
documentation:
Scale a point (typically a (width, height) pair or similar) that lies in pixel space to svg space. Only scaling is applied.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): point to scale

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: scaled point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.svg_scale_to_pixel_scale
documentation:
Scale a point (typically a (width, height) pair or similar) that lies in svg space to pixel space. Only scaling is applied.

Args:
    point (tuple[float, float]): point to scale

Returns:
    tuple[float, float]: scaled point

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.get_nowait
documentation:
Get all user input events that have happened since the last call to this method. Internally this will poll the `pygame` event loop.

Returns:
    list[Event]: user input events.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.is_open
documentation:
Is the window currently open?

Returns:
    bool: True if the window is open, False otherwise.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.close
documentation: Close the UI - terminate pygame and clean up.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.window_config
documentation:
Get current window configuration. This is a read only quantity, modifying it will have no effect, if you want to change any window property use the relevant `View` attributes.

Returns:
    WindowConfiguration: current window configuration.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.window_size
documentation:
Getter for the current window size.

Returns:
    tuple[int, int]: current window size

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View.window_size
documentation:
Setter for the window size. This operation is not cheap, it is not advisable to resize the window frequently.

Args:
    value (tuple[int,int]): new window size.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View._window_open_callback
documentation: Callback for when the pygame window is opened for the first time. A custom pygame event is added to the queue internally. The associated event timestamp is not accurate, but the event serves as an indication of when this view is ready to for updating/rendering.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View._window_focus_callback
documentation: Callback for `pywinctl` when the pygame window losses or gains focus. A custom pygame event is added to the queue internally.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View._window_moved_callback
documentation: Callback for `pywinctl` when the pygame window is moved. A custom pygame event is added to the queue internally.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View._window_resize_callback
documentation: Callback for `pywinctl` when the pygame window is resized. A custom pygame event is added to the queue internally.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View._screen_size_callback
documentation: Callback for when the screen size is discovered for the first time. A custom pygame event is added to the queue internally.

methods.name: star_ray_pygame.view.View._setup_pwc_window
documentation: Getter for the `pywinctl` window object. This object is used to watch changes to the pygame window and addresses some pygame limitations.
Class that an `Avatar` will use to render SVG in a pygame window and trigger/handle user input events.
